answer:
To construct a segment of length $\sqrt{7}$ using a ruler, we can use the properties of a regular hexagon.

Step 1: Draw a regular hexagon with a side length of 1.

Step 2: Draw the diagonal of the hexagon, which will have a length of 2 (since it is twice the side length).

Step 3: Draw a perpendicular line from the midpoint of the diagonal to the side of the hexagon. This will create a right triangle with legs of length 1 and 1.

Step 4: The hypotenuse of this right triangle will have a length of $\sqrt{2}$ (by the Pythagorean theorem).

Step 5: Draw a line from the midpoint of the diagonal to the midpoint of the side of the hexagon, which is also the midpoint of the hypotenuse of the right triangle. This will create another right triangle with legs of length 1 and $\sqrt{2}$.

Step 6: The hypotenuse of this right triangle will have a length of $\sqrt{3}$ (by the Pythagorean theorem).

Step 7: Draw a line from the midpoint of the diagonal to the midpoint of the hypotenuse of the right triangle, which is also the midpoint of the side of the hexagon. This will create another right triangle with legs of length 1 and $\sqrt{3}$.

Step 8: The hypotenuse of this right triangle will have a length of $\sqrt{4}$, which simplifies to 2.

Step 9: Draw a line from the midpoint of the diagonal to the midpoint of the hypotenuse of the right triangle, which is also the midpoint of the side of the hexagon. This will create another right triangle with legs of length 1 and 2.

Step 10: The hypotenuse of this right triangle will have a length of $\sqrt{5}$ (by the Pythagorean theorem).

Step 11: Draw a line from the midpoint of the diagonal to the midpoint of the hypotenuse of the right triangle, which is also the midpoint of the side of the hexagon. This will create another right triangle with legs of length 1 and $\sqrt{5}$.

Step 12: The hypotenuse of this right triangle will have a length of $\sqrt{6}$ (by the Pythagorean theorem).

Step 13: Draw a line from the midpoint of the diagonal to the midpoint of the hypotenuse of the right triangle, which is also the midpoint of the side of the hexagon. This will create another right triangle with legs of length 1 and $\sqrt{6}$.

Step 14: The hypotenuse of this right triangle will have a length of $\sqrt{7}$ (by the Pythagorean theorem).

Therefore, the segment whose length is $\sqrt{7}$ is the hypotenuse of the right triangle created in step 14.

\boxed{\sqrt{7}}
